name: English Garden
description: One of the world's largest urban parks. Great for picnics, cycling, and watching surfers at the Eisbach. Plenty of open space for kids to run.

name: Westpark Playground
description: Large playground with climbing frames, sand areas, and shaded spots. Popular with local families.

name: Hellabrunn Zoo
description: Family-friendly zoo with natural enclosures. Polar bears, elephants, and a petting area. Plan for half a day.

name: Deutsches Museum
description: Huge science and technology museum. Kids' Kingdom section is perfect for younger children. Good for rainy days.

name: Olympiapark
description: Olympic Park with lawns, lake, and climbing hill. Hosts events and has a small playground. Stroller-friendly paths.

name: Kinderreich at Deutsches Museum
description: Hands-on children's area inside Deutsches Museum. Water play, construction, and simple experiments. Book in advance.

name: Spielplatz am Flaucher
description: Playground by the Isar river with sand, climbing structures, and river access. Bring a towel in summer.

name: BMW Welt
description: Free exhibition space with cars and motorcycles. Interactive displays. Combine with Olympiapark next door.

name: Bavaria Filmstadt
description: Film studio tour with sets, stunt shows, and 4D cinema. Great for older kids who like movies and action.

name: Nymphenburg Palace Park
description: Baroque palace with large park and canals. Nice for walks and feed-the-ducks. Palace interior is optional.

name: Jugendmuseum
description: Children's museum with rotating hands-on exhibitions. Focus on history and culture in a child-friendly way.

name: Sea Life Munich
description: Aquarium in the Olympiapark area. Sharks, rays, and touch pool. Compact and manageable with toddlers.
